attempt.claim: Erdos #124: verified uniform equal-step identity construction for every admissible D with 3 in D, extending BEGL's single {3,4,7}. For such D and any k>=1, balanced ternary (Delta_3 = Z) yields disjoint P,Q subset S(D,k) supported on exponents >=N with sum Q - sum P = 1 (fixed step h=1, trivial residue reservoir). Verified computationally on 7 cases incl {3,4,5},{3,4,7},{3,5,8},{3,5,7,11}. This supplies the equal-step-identity ingredient of the interval-block lemma for all D containing 3 -- a real advance over the single known case -- but is NOT a full close: two gaps remain. (i) Syndetic-reservoir control: the interval-block criterion needs u+1 >= L_{F_u} where L_{F_u} is the deletion-syndeticity constant of S minus the u blocks, which may grow with u; a uniform bound or diagonal estimate is missing. (ii) For 3 not in D, balanced base-a Delta_a is a thin subset of Z, so the quotient-hit step fails -- the precise remaining obstruction.
attempt.detail: {"construction":"3 in D, gcd=1: pick b in D with 3 not| b; U = AP of b-powers with sum U = 1 mod 3^N; A=(1-sumU)/3^N; balanced ternary A=sum eps_t 3^t; Q=U cup {3^{N+t}:eps=1}, P={3^{N+t}:eps=-1}; sum Q - sum P = 1","extends":"BEGL {3,4,7} -> all admissible D containing 3","gaps":["syndetic-reservoir control (L_{F_u} may grow with u)","3 not in D: Delta_a thin for a>3 => quotient-hit lemma needed"],"provenance_note":"GPT-Pro construction; Opus verified sum Q - sum P = 1 on 7 cases + flagged the two gaps.","step":"h=1","verified":"scripts/verify_124_3inD_construction.py (7 admissible D with 3 in D)"}

caveats: A signed banked attempt. Trust is earned via its verifier_attachments + the gate, never from claimed_status.
